_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL House Pinellas batch HD-57..61: adam-anderson, kimberly-berfield, berny-jacques, lindsay-cross (D, NOT seeking 2026 reelection - announced 2026-06-04), linda-chaney. Votes verified against official flsenate.gov/myfloridahouse.gov roll-call PDFs + group endorsements + iVoterGuide/JBS Freedom Index/Digital Democracy Project.

profile.background: FL House HD-57 (Pinellas/Palm Harbor) since Nov 2022; reelected 2024; running 2026. Known for pediatric rare-disease programs named for his late son.

adam-anderson@FL.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: HARD roll-call YEAs on SB 300 (6-wk abortion ban), SB 254 (gender-care ban for minors), HB 1 (universal vouchers), HB 3 (porn age-verification), HB 931 (school chaplains), SB 1718 (E-Verify/sanctuary ban), SB 264 (foreign farmland); CO-INTRODUCER + YEA on HB 543 (permitless carry). iVoterGuide conservative profile (one-man/one-woman marriage, pro-life w/ exceptions, opposes PP funding, religious-conscience). Endorsed by police assoc + Florida Family Action. No cross-party deviation.

0.note: YEA on SB 300 6-week ban; iVoterGuide pro-life, opposes public funding of Planned Parenthood

4.note: Strongly opposes public funding for abortion providers incl. PP; no abortion-industry PAC money documented

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'Marriage is a God-ordained union of one man and one woman'

2.note: YEA on SB 254 banning gender-affirming care for minors

4.note: Opposes gender identity as a protected class

0.note: YEA on HB 1 universal school vouchers

1.note: YEA on SB 254 (parental control over minors' gender interventions)

3.note: YEA on HB 3 online age-verification protecting minors from porn

1.note: iVoterGuide: opposes forcing businesses to provide services violating religious beliefs

3.note: YEA on HB 931 volunteer school chaplains

4.note: YEA on SB 264 restricting foreign ownership of FL real property/farmland

0.note: Co-introducer and YEA on HB 543 permitless/constitutional carry

profile.background: FL House HD-58 (Pinellas) since Nov 2022; previously served HD-50 2000-2006. Insurance/healthcare professional; JBS Freedom Index 72%.

kimberly-berfield@FL.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: HARD roll-call YEAs on SB 300 (6-wk ban), SB 254 (gender-care ban), HB 3 (porn age-verification), HB 931 (chaplains), SB 1718 (sanctuary ban), HB 543 (permitless carry); co-sponsored HB 423 (2025 school choice); voted to roll back post-Parkland age-21 rifle limit (HB 1543/1223/759). Anti-ESG (HB 3 2023), sound-money (HB 999), SB 214 2A financial privacy, HB 601 anti-civilian-review (pro-police), HB 1451 (deny IDs to illegal migrants). NRA-endorsed 2022. Her HB 1 NO + HCR 693 NO track the JBS conservative position (tax-mechanism / anti-Art.V-convention), not progressive deviations.

4.note: No abortion-industry PAC funding documented; consistent pro-life voting record

2.note: YEA on SB 254 banning gender-affirming care for minors

YEA on HB 931 volunteer school chaplains

4.note: YEA on HB 3 (2023) Combating ESG Activism — anti-ESG

0.note: YEA on HB 601 stopping anti-police civilian review boards

2.note: YEA on SB 214 Second Amendment Financial Privacy — resists federal/financial surveillance of gun buyers

3.note: SB 1718 E-Verify; YEA on HB 1451 denying IDs to illegal migrants

1.note: Voted to roll back post-Parkland age-21 rifle limit (HB 1543/1223/759) — opposes age restriction

4.note: NRA-endorsed 2022; YEA on SB 214 blocking gun-purchase merchant-code tracking

profile.background: FL House HD-59 (Pinellas) since Nov 2022; running 2026. Haitian-born, former Assistant State Attorney (6th Circuit); NRA member.

berny-jacques@FL.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: YEA on SB 300 (6-wk ban); PRIMARY SPONSOR of pro-life HB 415 (pregnancy/parenting resources); Florida Right to Life endorsed 2024. PRIMARY SPONSOR HB 359 (hand-count ballots). Former prosecutor + Sheriff's Civil Service Board; heavy tough-on-crime sponsorship (career offenders, pretrial detention, death-penalty procedure). SPONSOR HB 1617 + HB 955 mandatory E-Verify; supported SB 1718. NRA member; co-sponsored HB 1543 gun-age rollback. SB 254/HB 1/HB 3/HB 543 individual roll-calls not independently confirmed -> left null (evidence discipline). No cross-party deviation.

0.note: YEA on SB 300 6-week ban; primary sponsor of pro-life HB 415 pregnancy-resources bill

4.note: Florida Right to Life endorsed 2024; no abortion-industry money

0.note: Primary sponsor of HB 359 authorizing precinct-level hand-counting and barring certain voting systems
Former Assistant State Attorney; appointed to Pinellas Sheriff's Civil Service Board; pro-law-enforcement record

1.note: Sponsored career-offender, pretrial-detention (HB 7067), and drive-by-shooting penalty bills — tough-on-crime, anti-soft-on-crime

2.note: Sponsored HB 1617, folded into SB 1718 sanctuary/immigration package

3.note: Primary sponsor HB 955 (2025) mandatory E-Verify for ALL private employers

1.note: Co-sponsored HB 1543 lowering long-gun purchase age back to 18 — opposes age restriction

4.note: NRA member; 'fierce defender of Constitutional rights'

profile.background: FL House HD-60 (St. Petersburg/Pinellas Park) since Nov 2022; Minority Deputy Whip. Environmental scientist. ANNOUNCED 2026-06-04 she will NOT seek reelection; seat is open in 2026 (term ends Nov 2026).

lindsay-cross@FL.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CANDIDACY: announced 2026-06-04 she will not seek reelection to HD-60 (open seat); remains sitting incumbent through Nov 2026. Documented progressive Democrat: NO on SB 300 (6-wk ban), supports Amendment 4, endorsed by Planned Parenthood/EMILY's List/Ruth's List; Equality Florida endorsement, NO on HB 1421/SB 1674; NO on HB 1 vouchers (FEA endorsed); NO on SB 1718 + SB 2B; NO on HB 543 permitless carry, Moms Demand 'Gun Sense Candidate'. CROSS-PARTY-ALIGNED CREDIT: voted YES on HB 1009 (2025) religious/patriotic expression in schools (note: passed 102-7, broadly bipartisan).

0.note: Voted NO on SB 300 6-week ban; campaigns on supporting Amendment 4 abortion-rights measure

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood PAC, EMILY's List, Ruth's List, Florida NOW PAC

1.note: Equality Florida endorsement; 'people should be free to love who they love' — supports same-sex marriage

2.note: Voted NO on HB 1421 gender-care restrictions and SB 1674 sex-based bathroom requirements

4.note: Opposes restrictions on LGBTQ+ in policy/schools; 'disgusted by bigoted attacks on the LGBTQ+ community'

0.note: Voted NO on HB 1 universal vouchers and HB 443 charter schools; FEA (teachers union) endorsed

3.note: Voted YES on HB 1009 (2025) religious & patriotic expression in public schools (documented credit; bill passed 102-7)

2.note: Voted NO on SB 1718 sanctuary ban and SB 2B immigration restrictions

0.note: Voted NO on HB 543 permitless/constitutional carry

1.note: Moms Demand 'Gun Sense Candidate'; backs 'common-sense gun reform' / universal background checks; NO on SB 214

profile.background: FL House since 2020 (HD-69, redistricted to HD-61, Pinellas/St. Pete Beach); running 2026. iVoterGuide conservative; NFIB-FL 90%.

linda-chaney@FL.notes_append: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: HARD roll-call YEAs verified against official FL House PDFs on HB 5 (15-wk ban, as Chaney-69), SB 300 (6-wk ban), SB 254 (gender-care ban), HB 1557 (Parental Rights/'Don't Say Gay'), HB 1 (vouchers), HB 931 (chaplains), SB 1718 (sanctuary ban); floor advocacy for 6-week ban; HB 289 fetal personhood YEA (2026). Endorsed by Florida Right to Life, NRA (2022), FOP, Florida Family Action. NOTE: a third-party scraper falsely showed her as NAY on SB 254 — official PDF confirms YEA. She did NOT vote on HB 543 permitless carry (absent — gap, not deviation). No cross-party deviation.
